geographic_scope: Srebrenica enclave at the time of takeover
excerpt: The page states that the Srebrenica enclave had approximately 42,000 inhabitants at the time of the takeover, about 36,000 of them refugees.
transform_note: Used only as contextual population-present evidence, not as a civilian person-month denominator for the execution complex.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence as a rounded tribunal-grounded contextual estimate.

geographic_scope: Srebrenica enclave shortly before/at the fall
excerpt: Human Rights Watch wrote that the population of the enclave had been reduced to approximately 40,000.
transform_note: Paired with the IRMCT figure to create a contextual 40,000-42,000 population-present range.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence as a rounded contextual estimate.

victim_side: Bosnian Muslims from the enclave, especially men and boys
inflicting_side: Bosnian Serb Army and police forces
excerpt: The IRMCT page says genocide was committed through the execution of more than 7,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys following the take-over of Srebrenica.
transform_note: Used as a conservative floor for direct deaths in the massacre complex.
confidence_note: High confidence as a tribunal-grounded lower bound.

victim_side: Persons missing from the fall of Srebrenica
inflicting_side: Bosnian Serb Army and police forces
excerpt: ICMP stated that it supported an estimate of around 8,100 individuals missing from the fall of Srebrenica in July 1995.
transform_note: Used as the best central estimate for the massacre complex, while recognizing that the source frames the number as missing from the fall rather than a completed status-coded death dataset.
confidence_note: Moderate-to-high confidence because it is linked to forensic identification work, but it is not a direct civilian/military split.

inflicting_side: Bosnian Serb Army and associated forces involved in the genocide
excerpt: The victim-list page states that it contains the names of 8,372 victims, mostly men and boys, killed in the Srebrenica genocide.
transform_note: Used as an upper-end named-victim benchmark, not as a status-coded civilian total.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence as a memorialized named-victim count.

excerpt: Human Rights Watch reported that 12,000 to 15,000 people joined the trek through the woods after the enclave fell.
transform_note: Context only; helps explain status ambiguity and why clean civilian/military allocation is unsafe.

excerpt: Human Rights Watch reported that of the 12,000 to 15,000 in the column, 3,000 to 4,000 were armed, while the majority were civilians.
transform_note: Status-ambiguity context only; not a death count.
